summary: The evidence supports a bounded mechanism claim that inter-agent interaction design creates failure pathways, while other core claims remain descriptive or deployment-bounded rather than causally explained.

bounded_claims.claim_text: Performance gains from the evaluated multi-agent LLM systems were minimal relative to single-agent frameworks and remained too weak for real-world deployment.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence supports a comparative performance result and a deployment bound, but it does not isolate a single causal mechanism for why gains stayed weak.

bounded_claims.claim_text: Multi-agent system failures are distributed across 14 distinct failure modes in 3 categories rather than being dominated by one top-level error class.
bounded_claims.bounds: This is a supported structural description of the failure landscape, but it does not by itself explain why each failure mode occurs.

bounded_claims.claim_text: In the ChatDev case study, prompt and topology interventions improved correctness from 25.0 to 34.4 and 40.6, but substantial failure remained after these best-effort changes.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence shows that orchestration changes can recover some performance, but the remaining low accuracy bounds any claim that these interventions explain or solve the underlying failures.
